def: s.m. The fifth month of the Hindūs (corresponding to a period in our calendar from about the middle of August to the middle of September), when the moon is full near the wing of Pegasus

Nor green in summer, nor dry in autumn. (Said of Prov. one who is neither rich nor poor). 2. Jo Bhādauṅ meṅ barkhā hoë, kāl pachhokaṛ jā-kar roë . Agric. Prov. When rains in August fall, fell famine weeps behind the wall. bhādoṅ kī bharan , n. f. Heavy August rains which fill the tanks, etc. and flood the fields. bhādoṅ ke ḍoṅgṛe, yā saṛakke , n. m. more: n. f. 1. An unlucky moment, as the second, seventh, and twelfth days of the lunar month. 1. Ghaṛī meṅ ghar jale, tīn ghaṛī bhadrā . Prov. In one half-hour the house in ashes see, 'in three half-hours the lucky time will be' (to put out the fire). A satire on the astrologers. 2. Shaving the head and whiskers. bhadrā honā , v. n. 1. To be clean or purified by shaving one's head and beard, etc. after the demise of a relative, or in a holy place. 2. ( Slang ). To be robbed or swept clean. Āj nīlām meṅ unkā bhadrā ho gayā .
s. m. Name of a bird, a lark.
s. f. 1. An unlucky moment. 2. The 2d, 7th, and 12th days of the lunar month.

def: adj. & s.m. Fixedness; firmness; determination; constancy. Raṅgīṅ, terī zabān ke nīche hai zabāṅ, Mutlaq terī bāt meṅ nahīṅ hai bhadrak . Wom. Rangīn. Rangiṅ, you have a tongue beneath your tongue you know, There's no believing you, you twist and double so. 4. Truth; truthfulness; exactness; credibility. 5. Gain; profit; advantage; benefit.
s. f. 1. Advantage, produce. 2. Nature, genius, reason. 3. Beauty, goodness, pleasantness.

s.m. Part, portion, member, share, lot, division; allotment, partition, distribution, apportionment; share in a partnership, or of an inheritance; share in kind; (in Arith.) division; tax, duty (as being the share of government); good portion, fortunate lot, good fortune, luck; fortune, lot, fate, destiny; — part of any whole, a fraction; a degree, or 360th part of the circumference of a circle
more: n. m. S. भाग्य from भज् to share. 1. Partition; apportionment; separate portion; a share in a partnership; a share or portion of an inheritance; a share in kind; the share of the Government. 2. ( Math .) Division (A. taqsīm ). 3. Fortune; lot; destiny; fate. Apnā apnā bhāg . 4. Good fortune; luck; prosperity. 1. Pher same ehu bane, na bane, gorī, ab-ke to khel le phāg rī; Dīn Dayāl se pritam kahāṅ mileṅ, dhan dhan apne bhāg rī ! Holī. Such luck may ne'er return, Love's feast enjoy we while we may, Like Krishn kind a Love where find, how blessed our fate to-day! 2. Rājā rūṭhegā apnā suhāg legā, kyā kisī kā bhāg legā? Prov. The offended king may put away his wife, but he cannot change her fate. 3. Nīk nīk more bhāg, ek ek machhalyā kī do do machhalyā . E. Prov. Good is my fate, for every fish doth two create. (Every rupee makes two). bhāg-bharā, bhāg-bharī , adj. 1. Fortunate; lucky; propi…
s. m. 1. Share, part, portion. 2. (or भाग्य ) Destiny, fate, fortune, lot. بهاگ بهروسا bhāg-bharosā, Consolation.

ety: bhāg˚ = Prk. भग्गे(इ), or भग्ग(इ), fr. भग्ग = S. भग्न, p.p.p. of rt. भंज्
def: v.n. To run, to run off or away, fly, flee, take flight, escape, abscond, make oneself scarce
more: v. n. S. भाज् to separate. 1. To run away; fly; flee; make off; give leg bail. 1. Āṅdhī āë baiṭh jāë, meṅh āë bhāg jāë . Prov. In a storm sit down, when it rains run away. 2. Tānā bānā jal * gayā, jalā nahīṅ ek tāgā, Ghar kā chor pakaṛ līyā, ghar morī meṅ ko bhāgā . Riddle. Ans. jāl a net. Both web and woof thrown in the jal , no single thread would burn, In it the thieves were caught, their house did through the drain excern. 3. Bijulik māral, luāṭh d ekhe bhāge . Prov. Singed by lightning he runs from a burning stick. 4. Āyā Ramzān, bhāgā Shaitān . Mah. Prov. When Ramzan (Mah. Lent) comes, the Devil flies. 5. Ūṅṭ jab bhāge, jab pachchham ko . Prov. When the camel bolts, it is to the West (to Arabia). (Home has the first place in the mind). 2. To elope or run away with. 3. To escape; abscond; make or effect one's escape. 4. To slip, steal, slink away; pack off; beat a retreat; turn tail…
v. n. To flee, to run away.
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀪𑀕𑁆𑀕𑀢𑀺 (*bhaggati), a verbal derivation from Sanskrit भग्न (bhagna, “broken, defeated, lost”), from the root भञ्ज् (bhañj). Cognate with Nepali भाग्नु (bhāgnu), Gujarati ભાગવું (bhāgvũ), as well as Punjabi ਭੱਜਣਾ (bhajjaṇā) from a different derivation.

(f. -ī), Good, excellent, virtuous, righteous; honest, respectable; benevolent, kind; healthy, well, sound; fortunate, prosperous; strange, wonderful, admirable; comical, droll; — adv. & intj. more: good. See اچّها adj. adv. and intj. 1. Bhale kā bhalā . Prov. Good yields good. 2. Bhale kī bāteṅ ras kī khān, Bure kī bāteṅ dukh nidhān . Prov. The words of good men are a hive of nectar sweet, The words of bad men are a nest of stings replete. bhale bure , n. m. Bad men; bad characters; rogues. bhalā burā kahnā, bhalī burī sunānā , v. a. To speak ill of; abuse; insult; reproach; inveigh against; rail at; vilify. bhalā chaṅggā , n. m. bhalī chaṅggī , n. f. See achchhā bichchhā. Bhalī chaṅgī apnī na kho jān tū, Ki woh hai parī, aur insān tū . Mir Hasan. bhalā karnā, bhalī karnā , v. a. See achchhā k . (1, 2). 1. Chaṛh jā beṭā sūlī par, Rām bhalī kareṅge! Prov. Come on my son, impale yourself, Ram will reward the sacrifice! (Said of sinister advice). 2. Bhalā kīyā so Khudā ne, burā kīyā so bande ne . Prov. Good is from God, evil from man. bhale ko , intj. How fortunate! how lucky! Be-jurm…

Adhan de-ke khojat rahalo, kehū na delā mohī pāiṅch rekī, Ser bhar chaūrā gotini mohe dīhalī, ūhī meṅ bhaīlo bhal mānas rekī! Wom. Bhoj. Song. The water set upon the fire grown hot, Some rice in vain on credit now I sought, A relative did then give me a seer, Which made me quite respectable appear! 2. A simple, meek, gentle, inoffensive person. Bhale mānas kī sab tarah kharābī hai . Prov. The good and pure all ills endure. (He is maligned whether he returns abuse for abuse, or bears it meekly). 3. n. m. S. भंज् to break. Breaking up; dissolution. bhanḍ kharābā, bhanḍak bhanḍā , n. m. A breaking up; dissolution; destruction; ruin. bhanḍ kar'nā , v. a. 1. Nahiṅ ṭalūṅ din rāt hāṭ se, baje kaṛākaṛ ḍanḍā, Ghaṛī ek meṅ dekh tamāshā, kar dūṅ bhanḍak bhanḍā . Sutre Shāhī.
